metadata.diagnosis: Atrial Fibrillation with Rapid Ventricular Response

presentation_variants.presenting_complaint: My heart has been racing all day

history_data.hpi: Patient developed sudden onset palpitations approximately 8 hours ago. Describes the heartbeat as fast and irregular. Associated with mild shortness of breath, fatigue and occasional dizziness. No syncope reported.
patient_quotes: It feels like my heart is skipping beats.
I can feel my heart racing.
I get lightheaded when I stand up.
Something feels off with my heartbeat.

investigations.ECG: Atrial fibrillation with rapid ventricular response at 148 bpm. Irregularly irregular rhythm. No visible P waves. No ST changes to suggest ischaemia.
investigations.Troponin: 0.02 ng/mL (within normal limits — no evidence of myocardial injury)
investigations.Chest X-Ray: Mild cardiomegaly. No pulmonary oedema. No pleural effusion.
investigations.Echocardiogram: Mildly dilated left atrium. Preserved LV systolic function, EF 58%. No valvular pathology identified.
investigations.TSH (Thyroid Function): 0.3 mIU/L (low — hyperthyroidism as possible precipitant; further thyroid workup indicated)
investigations.Urea & Electrolytes: Na 139 mmol/L, K 3.4 mmol/L (mild hypokalaemia — possible precipitant), Creatinine 88 µmol/L — otherwise normal
investigations.Magnesium: 0.68 mmol/L (low-normal — may contribute to arrhythmia; consider replacement)
investigations.CRP: 8 mg/L (normal — no evidence of acute infection or inflammation)
investigations.Coagulation Screen (PT/APTT/INR): PT 13s, APTT 28s, INR 1.1 — normal (patient not on warfarin at this episode)
investigations.Blood Glucose: 6.8 mmol/L — mildly elevated, consistent with known type 2 diabetes

hidden_diagnosis: Atrial Fibrillation with Rapid Ventricular Response

correct_diagnosis_keywords: atrial fibrillation with rapid ventricular response
management_actions: Rate control with beta blocker or diltiazem
